title: Set the data inlining row limit — set_inlining_row_limit
text: limit can set three levels (highest priority first): Table-level – persisted DuckLake metadata specific table Schema-level – persisted tables schema Global (DuckDB setting) – applies DuckLake connections Data inlining enabled default DuckLake v1.0 threshold 10 rows. insert delete affecting fewer rows limit written inlined table inside catalog instead creating Parquet file. streaming high-frequency-insert workloads, increase limit (e.g., 50 100). workloads always write large batches, default fine can disable inlining limit = 0. title: Execute code within a transaction — with_transaction

title: Execute code within a transaction — with_transaction
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Single operation with_transaction(   create_table(mtcars, "cars"),   author = "Data Team",   commit_message = "Add cars dataset" )  # Multiple operations in a block with_transaction({   create_table(mtcars, "cars")   create_table(iris, "flowers") }, author = "Data Team", commit_message = "Add datasets")  # With dplyr pipeline with_transaction(   get_ducklake_table("cars") |>     mutate(kpl = mpg * 0.425144) |>     replace_table("cars"),   author = "Data Team",   commit_message = "Add km/L column" )  # Automatic rollback on error tryCatch(   with_transaction({     create_table(mtcars, "cars")     stop("Simulated error")  # Transaction will be rolled back   }),   error = function(e) message("Transaction was rolled back: ", e$message) ) } # }

title: ducklake (development version)
text: New plotting functions, requiring suggested ggplot2 package shown action new “Visualizing Lake” vignette: plot_snapshots() draws table’s snapshot history commit-log timeline (snapshots order, authors, commit messages, inline markers long idle gaps) , without table name, whole lake swimlane one row per table; plot_table_changes() draws rows snapshot inserted, updated, deleted diverging bars; plot_table_files() draws table’s Parquet file count size disk. New get_table_info() returns per-table file statistics (data delete file counts sizes) DuckLake catalog, wrapping DuckLake’s ducklake_table_info() function. New targeted maintenance wrappers complement checkpoint_ducklake(): expire_snapshots() (older_than, versions, dry_run), merge_adjacent_files(), cleanup_old_files(), delete_orphaned_files(), rewrite_data_files() (#16, suggested @stefanlinner). New partitioning support: set_table_partitioning() reset_table_partitioning() manage table’s partition keys (identity, year/month/day/hour, bucket transforms), get_table_partitions() lists keys metadata catalog (#16, suggested @stefanlinner). New get_table_changes() exposes DuckLake’s data change feed: exact inserts, deletes, update pre/post images two snapshots, lazy table composes dplyr verbs (#16, suggested @stefanlinner). Timestamps passed new functions POSIXct converted UTC interpolation, matching DuckLake records snapshot times. get_ducklake_table_asof() restore_table_version() now also convert POSIXct timestamps UTC. Previously rendered local time, DuckLake reads UTC, silently shifting queried instant UTC offset – bare Sys.time() looked hours past (future) unless session’s timezone UTC. Timestamps taken list_table_snapshots()$snapshot_time unaffected. attach_ducklake() now collapses duplicate slashes lake_path (remote URIs untouched). DuckLake compares file paths exact strings, doubled slash – R’s tempdir() produces macOS – made delete_orphaned_files() treat every live data file orphaned. dplyr--DuckLake translation behind ducklake_exec() show_ducklake_query() now built dbplyr’s structured query objects (dbplyr::sql_build()) instead pattern-matching rendered SQL text. Classification longer depends SQL happens look like, fixes several latent bugs: filtered read another table (get_ducklake_table("staging") |> filter(...) |> ducklake_exec("target")) translated DELETE target table; now appends matching rows, intended. Filter values containing SQL keywords (e.g. filter(note != "")) refused “complex”; now translate fine. mutate() adds new column refused upfront pointer replace_table(), instead failing database binder error. INSERT translations now list columns explicitly, appends another table match columns name rather position, joined unioned sources can appended one step. Pipelines compile subquery target table (grouped filters, filtering just-mutated column), clauses -place equivalent (arrange(), head(), distinct()), detected structurally refused clear message rather mistranslated. dbplyr (>= 2.5.0) now required; dbplyr 2.5.x select-list format introduced dbplyr 2.6.0 supported.

title: ducklake 0.4.0
text: release focuses production hardiness: self-contained connection management, working detach/restore, SQL identifier safety, Quack remote access, documentation overhaul.

title: ducklake 0.4.0
text: Added support Quack, DuckDB’s client-server protocol, became core extension DuckDB 1.5.3 (#20, @JavOrraca). DuckLake served one DuckDB instance can now queried modified R sessions network. concurrent access lighter-weight option PostgreSQL SQLite catalog, since whole setup stays inside DuckDB DuckLake. attach_quack() connects remote Quack server attaches catalog current session. detach_quack() disconnects remote Quack server. install_quack() installs Quack DuckDB extension. quack_query() runs one-query remote Quack server returns data.frame. quack_serve() serves current session, including attached DuckLake, clients Quack. quack_stop() stops running Quack server.

title: ducklake 0.4.0
text: attach_ducklake() gains encrypted argument: pass encrypted = TRUE DuckLake encrypt Parquet files writes (#18). Note encryption keys stored catalog database, protect catalog. httpfs extension loaded automatically encrypted lakes, since platforms (notably Windows) DuckDB’s built-crypto module read-. restore_table_version() now works. previously generated RESTORE TABLE statement exist DuckLake failed every call. now recreates table time-travel read inside transaction, recording restore new snapshot history preserved. also gains author commit_message arguments restore snapshot carries full audit-trail metadata. get_ducklake_backend() gains ducklake_name argument tracks attached lake separately, sessions several lakes different catalog backends resolve backend-specific behaviour correctly.

title: ducklake 0.4.0
text: detach_ducklake() now actually detaches. Previously DETACH ran lake still session’s current database, DuckDB refuses, error silently swallowed – lake stayed attached. session now switches back connection’s catalog first. Relatedly, restoring backup new location requires override_data_path = TRUE (documented); storage vignette example corrected. Table names, lake names, file paths now quoted validated interpolated SQL (DBI::dbQuoteIdentifier() friends), names spaces quotes longer produce malformed statements. rows_insert(), rows_update(), rows_delete() now also dispatch S3 methods tables returned get_ducklake_table(). Previously, dplyr loaded ducklake, dplyr’s generics masked ducklake’s wrappers calls failed conflict = "error" complaints; load order longer matters. rows_insert(), rows_update(), rows_delete() now work inside with_transaction(), several row operations can grouped single snapshot author commit message. Previously, passing local data frame made dbplyr copy temporary table inside transaction, DuckDB rejects one already open. Local data frames now sent inline queries (dbplyr::copy_inline()), also faster small changesets functions designed . backup_ducklake() backs every schema directory, just main. create_table() now converts factor columns character (message) instead failing “unsupported type ENUM” – DuckLake support DuckDB’s ENUM type, factors become. internal dplyr--SQL translation ducklake_exec() longer uses sink() (leak diverted output error), now refuses queries subqueries multiple clauses instead generating incorrect SQL. ducklake_exec() longer executes statement twice. internal translation step also executed SQL ducklake_exec() ran , every call created two snapshots non-idempotent updates (e.g. v = v + 1) applied twice. show_ducklake_query() now true preview: previously executed translated statement lake displaying . ducklake_exec() now translates mutate() UPDATE, just compile CASE . Previously simple transformation like mutate(v = round(v, 1)) fell INSERT table’s rows, silently duplicating table. Plain self-reads nothing translate now refused reason, UPDATE assignments containing commas inside function calls parsed correctly. list_table_snapshots(table_name) longer misses snapshots created rows_insert(), rows_update(), rows_delete(). DuckLake records row-level changes table’s numeric id rather name; filter now resolves matches ids, per-table audit trail complete. Filtered listings also number rows 1 instead leaking row positions unfiltered result.

title: ducklake 0.4.0
text: ducklake now creates manages DuckDB connection instead reaching duckplyr’s unexported internals. removes package’s last ::: calls duckplyr dependency entirely.

title: ducklake 0.4.0
text: set_ducklake_connection() (returning popular demand, now safer): point ducklake DuckDB connection manage — example one shared DBI tools. Connections supply never closed ducklake; automatically created connection shut detach_ducklake(shutdown = TRUE) session exit.

title: ducklake 0.3.0
text: release aligns package DuckLake v1.0 stable specification, requires DuckDB v1.5.2+ (compatible duckdb R package >= 1.5.1).

title: ducklake 0.3.0
text: DuckDB version requirement bumped 1.3.0 1.5.1 (duckdb R package) / 1.5.2 (DuckDB engine/CLI) match DuckLake v1.0. install_ducklake() now enforces engine level. commit_transaction() with_transaction() now use official CALL ducklake.set_commit_message() API set commit metadata within transaction COMMIT, consistent v1.0 specification. set_snapshot_metadata() retroactively updates ducklake_snapshot_changes metadata table directly.

title: ducklake 0.2.0
text: DuckLake now supports PostgreSQL, SQLite, MySQL catalog backends addition DuckDB (#15, @stefanlinner). aligns DuckLake 1.0 specification enables concurrent multi-client access using PostgreSQL SQLite.

title: ducklake 0.2.0
text: attach_ducklake() gains backend, catalog_connection_string, read_only, override_data_path parameters multi-backend support. install_ducklake() gains backend parameter pre-install backend extensions (e.g., install_ducklake(backend = "postgres")). New get_ducklake_backend() returns active catalog backend type. detach_ducklake() gains shutdown parameter. default now performs soft detach (SQL DETACH + USE memory;) instead shutting connection, allowing backend switching within session. backup_ducklake() now backend-aware: file-based backends (DuckDB, SQLite) get catalog + data copied; PostgreSQL/MySQL get data guidance use pg_dump/mysqldump. Also fixes pre-existing bug catalog backups silently 0 bytes due DuckDB holding file locks file.copy().

title: ducklake 0.2.0
text: attach_ducklake() now requires lake_path (previously optional). set_ducklake_connection() removed. package now exclusively uses duckplyr’s singleton DuckDB connection. detach_ducklake() longer shuts DuckDB connection default. Pass shutdown = TRUE previous behaviour.

title: ducklake 0.2.0
text: Schema qualifier logic updated throughout (get_metadata_table(), time_travel.R, transactions.R) handle PostgreSQL/MySQL backends don’t use .main. schema prefix. New internal helpers: build_attach_sql(), ensure_extensions(), shutdown_and_reset_singleton().
